message:
feat(db-runtime): Add in-memory caching for bias and codon frequency vectors

Extend the PhageRepository interface and BunSqliteRepository implementation
with optional methods for caching computed dinucleotide and codon frequency
vectors, avoiding expensive recomputation on overlay re-renders.

Changes to packages/db-runtime/src/types.ts:
- Added four optional methods to PhageRepository interface:
  - getBiasVector(phageId): Retrieve cached 16-element dinucleotide vector
  - setBiasVector(phageId, vector): Store dinucleotide vector
  - getCodonVector(phageId): Retrieve cached 64-element codon vector
  - setCodonVector(phageId, vector): Store codon vector
- All methods are optional (marked with ?) for backward compatibility

Changes to packages/db-runtime/src/bun-repository.ts:
- Implemented all four caching methods using the existing LRU cache:
  - getBiasVector: Looks up 'bias:{phageId}' in cache
  - setBiasVector: Stores with 'bias:{phageId}' key and current timestamp
  - getCodonVector: Looks up 'codonvec:{phageId}' in cache
  - setCodonVector: Stores with 'codonvec:{phageId}' key

- Fixed floating promise warning: Added 'void' prefix to fire-and-forget
  getPhageById() call in prefetchNeighbors() to suppress linter warning

Cache Implementation Notes:
- Uses existing in-memory LRU cache (no SQLite persistence)
- Cache keys prefixed with 'bias:' and 'codonvec:' to namespace
- Timestamp stored for potential TTL expiration (future enhancement)
- Vectors stored as raw number[] arrays
- Returns null if not cached, enabling callers to compute and store

This enables the BiasDecompositionOverlay to persist computed frequency
vectors across overlay open/close cycles without recomputing from full
genome sequences each time.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-92s","title":"Foundation: Progressive Disclosure & User Journey","description":"## Progressive Disclosure & User Journey

Strategy for exposing features based on user expertise level.

### New User Journey (First 5 minutes)
- Only Layer 0 (12 sacred controls)
- Help overlay shows essential controls only
- Navigate phages, scroll sequences, toggle DNA/AA

### Intermediate User (First hour)
- Layer 0 + Layer 1 overlays
- Discover overlays via help
- Try diff mode, explore 3D model
- Help now shows 'Advanced: press A for more...'

### Power User
- Full Layer 0-4 access
- Uses command palette (: or Ctrl+P)
- Runs simulations, does comparative analysis
- Discovers features through exploration

### Key Insight
Every feature reachable in ≤3 keypresses but invisible until needed.

Depends on: Help System (phage_explorer-ofm), UI Design System (phage_explorer-dx3)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:35.931114-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ofm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:01.879645-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-dx3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:02.988977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
